arts.ttl: Man United fans finally get something to cheer about as Ruben Amorim's men show signs of life to thwart Arsenal, writes IAN LADYMAN
arts.sum: IAN LADYMAN: The stark truth about most football fans is that they are malleable. Give them something to cheer on the field and they will go home happy and turn up again next time round.

arts.ttl: Papers: Man Utd have 12-month option on Mainoo future
arts.sum: The top stories and transfer rumours from Monday's newspapers...

arts.ttl: Roy Keane sends Arsenal devastating home truths in angry rant after Man United draw
arts.sum: Former Manchester United captain Roy Keane has told Arsenal to worry about teams directly below them in a blistering rant following the Gunners' draw at Old Trafford on Sunday. "I am not seeing an Arsenal team turning up today and thinking: ‘I tell you what, they were machine-like, they knew United were there for the taking.' They were not even ready to take that step.

arts.ttl: Analysis: Attacking issues remain for Man Utd and Arsenal
arts.sum: "We tried to choose the best moments to press high, and we created chances to win the game," Amorim told Sky Sports, explaining his side's second-half advances. For 45 minutes, Arsenal weren’t confronted at all until they entered the final third, a novel sight for United supporters at Old Trafford and perhaps a sign Amorim saw how PSV Eindhoven conceded seven at home to the Gunners after opening up in midweek.

arts.ttl: Anthony Taylor Made Error Which Helped Manchester United vs Arsenal
arts.sum: Christian Eriksen revealed he had noticed a crucial mistake from referee Anthony Taylor prior to Man United's goal in the 1-1 draw with Arsenal.

arts.ttl: Liverpool shock Arsenal to set up FA Cup semi-final against Chelsea
arts.sum: The other semi-final will see City meet United in a Manchester derby

arts.ttl: Roy Keane clashes with Jamie Redknapp over Arsenal’s title hopes
arts.sum: The Gunners’ 1-1 draw with United means they now trail Liverpool by 15 points at the top of the table

arts.ttl: Mikel Arteta gives verdict on Arsenal's wall blunder in Man United draw after Bruno Fernandes took advantage to fire in Red Devils opener
arts.sum: CHRIS WHEELER: Mikel Arteta has given his verdict on Arsenal's wall being more than a yard further back than it had to be when Bruno Fernandes fired Manchester United ahead from a free kick.

arts.ttl: Ruben Amorim reacts to Manchester United fan protests outside Old Trafford
arts.sum: Supporters marched to Old Trafford in protest against the club’s ownership

arts.ttl: Man United sent new referee VAR verdict as Anthony Taylor Arsenal penalty decisions explained
arts.sum: Anthony Taylor chose not to award Arsenal a penalty in their clash against Manchester United on Sunday
